19.reason: [The loop condition should check for EOF, not -1. scanf returns EOF on end-of-file or error, not -1 for the input value -1. This causes the program to not terminate correctly when -1 is read.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for EOF, not -1. scanf returns EOF on end-of-file or error, not -1 for the input value -1. This causes the program to not terminate correctly when -1 is read.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The loop condition should check for EOF, not -1. When scanf fails (e.g., at end of input), it returns EOF, not -1. This causes the loop to not terminate properly when -1 is read as an operation, because op becomes -1 and the loop condition evaluates to false (since scanf returns 1 on successful read of -1), but then the else clause handles -1 correctly. However, the real issue is that after reading -1, the program exits, so this may not be the main bug. The actual critical bug is in the Error function using puts(), which appends a newline.]
